title: Salted Caramel Sauce
intro: Diana Henry writes some of the most beautiful food books around. This salted caramel is glossy and deep — and the Thermomix makes it without the fear of a burnt pan.
steps: Place the sugar and water in the bowl. Cook 9 min / Varoma / speed 1, MC off, until deep amber.
Add the butter and cook 2 min / 100°C / speed 2 (take care, it will bubble up).
Add the cream and salt and mix 30 sec / 80°C / speed 3 until smooth and glossy. Cool — it thickens as it sits.
tips: Watch the colour at the end — amber is flavour, but too dark turns bitter.
Keeps 2 weeks in the fridge; warm gently to pour.

faq.q: Is it hard to make caramel in a Thermomix?
faq.a: No — it cooks at a steady temperature with no stirring, so there's far less risk of burning.

faq.q: Is this the original Diana Henry recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Diana Henry and salted caramel; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Salted Caramel Sauce in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.

description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Diana Henry-style salted caramel: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
